zh.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery是Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery的兒子，於1295年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達卡布瑞。他和carew至少有2個兒子。他去世於1320年在他的家鄉享年25歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery是Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery的儿子，于1295年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達卡布瑞。他和carew至少有2个儿子。他去世于1320年在他的家乡享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Cuong, Minh, Lien, Muoi, Thanh, Tho, Tieu, Vinh, Anh, Beo, Binh, Chau.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 麥 (see   Mai   2) and 莫 (see   Mo   1).
Vietnamese (Mạc): from Chinese 莫 ‘nothing; nonexistent’; see Mo 1.

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Carthy
meanings: Car, dear, and tir, a country, and ać for neać, an individual, originally cartać, meaning a beloved countryman
Irish: A beloved countryman

nameMeanings.name: Reagh,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Ute.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Riabhach, which MacLysaght describes as an adjectival surname (meaning ‘gray’ or ‘brindled’) that ‘took the place of an hereditary one’.
Possibly an altered form of German   Rech  .

variants.name: Rech
meanings: German:
from an ancient Germanic personal name, Recho, a short form of a personal name composed with rāhha ‘revenge’.
variant of   Reh   ‘roe deer’.

nameMeanings.name: Prince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: nickname from Middle English, Old French prince (from Latin princeps).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Prinz ‘prince’, or its Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or some other Slavic cognate Princ, from princ ‘prince’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Primc: status name for one who lives in the house of his father-in-law, from a derivative of primiti ‘to accept, to take over’.

variants.name: Prinz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Matthias, Ulrich, Arno, Friedrich, Hans, Klaus, Mechthild.

German: nickname from Middle High German prinze ‘prince’, presumably denoting someone who behaved in a regal manner or who had won the title in some contest of skill. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German Prinz ‘prince’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Carbery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish, Irish:  see  Carberry  .

variants.name: Carberry
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from a place in the parish of Inveresk, Lothian, first recorded in the form Crebarrin, from Gaelic craobh ‘tree’ + barran ‘hedge’.
Irish (Waterford and Wexford, Armagh) and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cairbre and Mac Cairbre ‘descendant of Cairbre’ and ‘son of Cairbre’, a byname probably meaning ‘charioteer’.

nameMeanings.name: Donal
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Earlier Domhnall , from Old Celtic dubno ‘world’ + val ‘rule’.

variants.name: Donall
meanings: Earlier Domhnall , from Old Celtic dubno ‘world’ + val ‘rule’.

nameMeanings.name: Caomh,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Recent coinage, from a word meaning ‘holy’ or ‘saint’.

nameMeanings.name: "the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

en.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery was born in 1295, in Carbury, County Kildare, Ireland as the son of Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery. He had at least 2 sons with carew. He died in 1320, in his hometown, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery est né en 1295, à Carbury, County Kildare, Irlande, fils de Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec carew. Il est décédé en 1320, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery wurde 1295 in Carbury, County Kildare, Irland, als Sohn von Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery geboren. Er hatte mit carew mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1320 im Alter von 25 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery è nato il nel 1295 nel Carbury, County Kildare, Irlanda come figlio di Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con carew. Morì nel 1320, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 25 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carberyは1295年にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアカーブリーでDonal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carberyの息子として生まれた。彼はcarewとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1320年に故郷で25歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery(은)는 1295년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 카버이에서 Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 carew(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1320년 그의 고향에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery nasceu em 1295, em Carbury, County Kildare, Irlanda filho de Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com carew. Ele faleceu em 1320, em sua cidade natal, com 25 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery родился в 1295 г. в местности карбури, коунти каилдар, Ирландия. Его отца звали Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от carew. Он умер в 1320 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Donal Caomh, "the Handsome", Mac Carthy Reagh, Prince of Carbery nació en 1295, en Carbury, County Kildare, Irlanda como hijo de Donal Maol Mac Carthy Reagh, Lord of Carbery. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con carew. Murió en 1320, en su pueblo, a la edad de 25 años.
